Description: TARS shall enter a Safe-stop on any detected critical fault and shall inhibit movement until fault clearance
TARS shall mantain constant communication with a base control station during operation
TARS shall have five operation modes: AUTONOMOUS, TELEOPERATED, ENABLED, DISABLED, and OFF
TARS shall inhibit all actuator motion commands unless the system is in ENABLED and either AUTONOMOUS or TELEOPERATED mode
TARS shall indicate operation mode in a color-agnostic fashion
TARS shall have a base station that displays all relevant telemetry
The base station of TARS shall be designated B0 where applicable
TARS shall have a base station that has an interface to send commands to individual coprocessor legs
TARS shall have a base station that has an emergency software shutoff system
TARS shall have two 5-inch touchscreen displays located on the two inner front legs
TARS shall indicate the current operation mode on at least one of the front displays
TARS shall recieve movement objectives as standardized commands from the base station
TARS shall use 3D printed PETG parts for prototype brackets, cable management, and component mounts where practical
TARS shall use a Jeston Orin Nano as the primary on-board computer
The primary on-board computer for TARS shall be located in the rightmost leg
TARS shall use three Raspberry pi zero w microcontrollers as coprocessors
TARS shall use coprocessors to aggregate accelerometer data
TARS shall have overall dimensions: Height: 30 inches, Width: 15 inches, Depth: 3.75 inches
Each leg of TARS shall have overall dimensions: Height: 30 inches, Width: 3.75 inches, Depth: 3.75 inches
TARS shall implement Safe-stop by a) commanding actuators to a non-driving disabled state, and b) asserting a software inhibit that blocks further actuation
TARS shall publish telemetry including operation mode, fault status, battery statys, IMU status, and actuator status to the base station at an update rate greater than or equal to 20Hz
TARS shall enter Safe-stop upon loss of base station communications for longer than a defined timeout
TARS shall perform all necessary movement calculations on the on-board Jetson Orin Nano
TARS shall power each leg independently with a 6S 5000 mAh battery
TARS shall operate from a nominal 6S LiPo battery supply
TARS shall use 14 AWG wire for main battery and actuator power distribution where current requires
TARS shall use 22-26 AWG wire for data transfer, as determined by the Electrical team
TARS shall terminate power wiring using crimped connectors or ferrules appropriate for terminal type
TARS shall use keyed detachable connectors for battery input and servo power harnesses
TARS shall provue overcurent protection on the main battery feed using an inline fuse
TARS shall generate a regulated 5V electronics supply rail from the 6S input using a DC buck converter
TARS shall have four legs, each labeled L0-3
L1 of TARS shall hold the main computer
L0 of TARS shall contrain coprocessor, will have Raspberry Pi Pico
L2 of TARS shall contain coprocessor, will have Raspberry Pi 0W
L3 of TARS shall contain coprocessor, will have Raspberry Pi Pico
Each coprocessor of TARS shall be responsible for aggregating data from the sensors in that leg
Each coprocessor of TARS shall independently transmit telemetry to the central computer via USB bus
Each coprocessor of TARS shall connect to B0
Each coprocessor of TARS shall recieve commands via USB bus
Each coprocessor of TARS shall recieve commands via B0
The central computer of TARS shall recieve commands from B0
Direct manipulation of TARS shall be enacted via the central computer, except for in instances of testing
TARS shall support wireless software updates via connection to B0
Each leg of TARS shall independently listen for a leg-specific emergency stop from B0
Each leg of TARS shall independently listen for a global software E-stop signal from B0
B0 shall have an interface for command-line access to each coprocessor and the central computer within TARS
TARS shall collect rotational position of each linear screw as telemetry
TARS shall actuate the active carriage of each leg via linear screw driven by a NEMA-17 stepper motor
TARS shall actuate the passive carriage of each leg via linear screw driven by a NEMA-17 stepper motor
TARS shall report rotational position of each leg as telemetry
TARS shall calculate rotational position of each leg as an aggregate position from three MPU 6050 accelerometer/gyroscope chips using the madgewick AHRS algorithm
TARS shall calculate linear position of the active and passive carriages as a function of the reported position from rotary encoders
TARS shall have a rotary encoder attached to one end of the main lead screw rod
Each leg of TARS shall identify its role within TARS as a function of the available components and physical position on the USB bus
TARS shall use ROS Kilted as a primary code framework
TARS shall use python as the primary programming language
TARS shall boot into DISABLED and require explicit ENABLE
E-stop shall require deliberate reset action and shall not auto-reenable
